preferences.raw_input: Plan a 4-day solo backpacking trip to Rishikesh under ₹15,000. I love adventure sports and spiritual experiences. Traveling from Delhi next weekend. I prefer budget stays and local food.

categories.description: Delhi → Haridwar → Rishikesh by Jan Shatabdi + shared cab

budget_breakdown.optimization_notes: Chose dormitory stay over private rooms to save ₹1,200. Using shared cabs instead of private taxis saves ₹400. Main splurge is the 16km rafting stretch which is worth every rupee.

transport_booking.name: Jan Shatabdi Express (12055)
transport_booking.description: Delhi → Haridwar, then shared cab Haridwar → Rishikesh (1 hr, ₹150)
transport_booking.notes: Book 2 weeks ahead; Haridwar station to Rishikesh by shared auto or cab

hotel_booking.name: Zostel Rishikesh
hotel_booking.description: India's most loved backpacker hostel in Tapovan area with river views, rooftop cafe, and curated community events
hotel_booking.notes: Book 1 week in advance; dorm beds & private rooms; great for solo travelers

activity_bookings.name: White Water Rafting (16 km)
activity_bookings.description: Shivpuri to Rishikesh stretch through Grade 3-4 rapids – most popular rafting experience
activity_bookings.notes: Helmet and life jacket provided; best done morning or early afternoon

activity_bookings.name: Ganga Aarti at Triveni Ghat
activity_bookings.description: Mesmerizing fire ceremony on the banks of the Ganges at sunset – a deeply spiritual experience
activity_bookings.notes: Arrive 30 mins early for front row spot; starts at 6 PM in winter, 7 PM in summer

activity_bookings.name: Beatles Ashram (Chaurasi Kutia)
activity_bookings.description: Iconic ashram where The Beatles studied TM meditation in 1968 – now an open-air art gallery
activity_bookings.notes: Photography allowed; wear comfortable shoes; open 9 AM – 5 PM

activity_bookings.name: Neer Garh Waterfall Trek
activity_bookings.description: Short 2.5 km trek through forest to a pristine waterfall – great for nature lovers
activity_bookings.notes: Best visited morning before 11 AM; carry water and snacks

blocks.description: Board Jan Shatabdi Express from New Delhi / Haridwar Junction. Grab breakfast at station.
blocks.tips: Book 2F or window seat for scenic Himalayan foothills views

blocks.description: Arrive Haridwar, take shared cab or Vikram to Rishikesh (45–60 min). Lunch on the way.
blocks.tips: Shared Vikrams leave from outside Haridwar station. Negotiate ₹100-150.

blocks.description: Check in, freshen up, meet fellow travelers at the common area. Drop your bag.
blocks.tips: Say hi to the hostel staff for local tips and free itinerary advice

blocks.description: Walk to Triveni Ghat for the mesmerizing sunset fire Aarti. Hundreds of oil lamps float on the Ganges.
blocks.tips: Arrive by 5:30 PM for a good spot. Remove shoes. Incredibly peaceful experience.

blocks.description: Famous local restaurant near Triveni Ghat known for North Indian thalis and dal makhani.
blocks.tips: Try the Special Thali for ₹180-220. Also try the lassi!

blocks.description: Fuel up with a hearty breakfast. Scrambled eggs, toast, chai. Pack a snack for the river.
blocks.tips: Wear clothes you don't mind getting wet. Waterproof your valuables.

blocks.activity: White Water Rafting – Shivpuri to Rishikesh
blocks.description: 16 km exhilarating rafting through Grade 3-4 rapids: Golf Course, Three Blind Mice, Body Surfing! Equipment & guide included.
blocks.tips: Book the night before or early morning. Waterproof pouches for phone (₹50 rental). Listen to your guide!

blocks.description: Post-rafting lunch at one of the cafes near Ram Jhula. Great views, hearty meals.
blocks.tips: Try the maggi noodles + chai combo – ultra satisfying post-adventure!

blocks.description: Several spots near Lakshman Jhula offer natural cliff jumping into the Ganga. Thrilling!
blocks.tips: Only jump at designated spots with locals present. River can be strong.

blocks.description: Stroll across the iconic suspension bridge, browse local bead & gem shops, watch the sunset.
blocks.tips: Great photo ops at golden hour. Try the banana lassi from street stalls.

blocks.description: Rooftop cafe with Ganga views, great for pasta, momos, and local curries.
blocks.tips: Arrive before sunset for the best view table

blocks.description: Quick breakfast at hostel – banana pancakes and coffee
blocks.tips: Start early to beat the heat on the trek

blocks.description: 2.5 km forest trail leads to a stunning multi-tiered waterfall. Refreshing dip at the base!
blocks.tips: Wear sports shoes. Carry water bottle. The upper falls are less crowded.

blocks.description: Explore the abandoned ashram where John Lennon, Paul McCartney, Ringo Starr & George Harrison came in 1968. Beautiful murals throughout.
blocks.tips: Entry ₹150 for Indians. Spend time reading the history boards. Meditation dome is magical.

blocks.description: Beachside seating by the Ganga, cold drinks, light meals
Drop-in yoga class at one of the most famous ashrams on the Ganga bank. Transformative experience.
blocks.tips: Wear comfortable clothes. Drop-in classes available. Check timings at the gate.

blocks.description: Dinner at Cafe Nirvana with rooftop stargazing over the Himalayas
blocks.tips: The pumpkin soup is famous here. Great spot for meeting fellow travelers.

blocks.description: Wake up early and meet your shared taxi at the hostel gate.
blocks.tips: Book the taxi the night before. ₹600 for a shared jeep (4-5 people)

blocks.description: 25 km drive up to the Kunjapuri Devi Temple at 1,676m elevation. Breathtaking 360° Himalayan sunrise view – Gangotri, Kedarnath peaks visible.
blocks.tips: Carry a warm jacket! It's cold at 5 AM. Worth every rupee.

blocks.description: Last breakfast in Rishikesh. Check out of Zostel and store bags if needed.
blocks.tips: Buy some local honey or Rudraksha souvenirs from the market

blocks.description: Shared cab from Rishikesh to Haridwar station for your return train.
blocks.tips: Leave by 10 AM for a 12 PM train from Haridwar

blocks.description: Jan Shatabdi back to Delhi. Great time to journal your trip memories!
blocks.tips: Book return ticket in advance. Carry snacks for the journey.

booking_checklist: Book Delhi → Haridwar train on IRCTC (Jan Shatabdi or Mussoorie Express)
Reserve rafting slot via Thrillophilia a day in advance
Arrange Kunjapuri sunrise taxi through hostel (night before)
Book return Haridwar → Delhi train ticket
insider_tips: Tapovan area is quieter than Ram Jhula – better for budget stays and great cafes
Bargain with local auto-rickshaws; always agree on price before boarding
Rishikesh is alcohol-free – respect local customs
ATMs near Ram Jhula but carry cash for small vendors
Best month to visit: March-May and September-November. Monsoon brings flood risk.
weather_summary: Pleasant and cool in Feb–March. Expect 12-22°C. Carry light layers and a windbreaker for evenings.
